member.office_address: 203 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3203

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please only include one funding request per form submission. If you are requesting funding for more than one project, please fill out the form multiple times. My office's internal deadline is March 6, 2026. After submitting your application, we may reach out with supplemental questions. For assistance, please refer to our Constituent Application Guide, which you can find here: https://suozzi.house.gov/services/appropriations. If you have specific questions, please contact my DC office at (202) 225-3335, or email Cindy Rogers at Cindy.Rogers@mail.house.gov.

fields.label:
Appropriations requests typically fall under one of three categories:

Funding Requests:�requests a funding level for an authorized program.�Bill Language Requests: requests legal language to be included directly in an appropriations bill.�Report Language Requests: requests language to be inserted into the Appropriations Committee Report. 

Which category does your request fall under?
options: Funding Request
Bill Language Request
Report Language Request
fields.rationale: The request specifies a $9 billion funding level for an existing authorized program (NASA SMD).

fields.rationale: The FY2026 enacted level for NASA Science is $7.250 billion.

fields.rationale: The President's Budget Request for FY2027 has not yet been released.

fields.label: Your Funding Request
fields.rationale: The strategic funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate is $9 billion.

fields.label: Location of impact in NY-03 or NYS more broadly
fields.draft_value: NY-03 and New York State broadly
fields.rationale: NASA Science investments reach NY-03, including local entities like B&S Aircraft Alloys Inc. which has received contracts totaling over $94,000.

fields.label: Explanation/justification of request, please include how your request would benefit NY-03 or NYS
fields.draft_value: This $9 billion request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate ensures U.S. leadership in space exploration and economic competitiveness. Every dollar invested returns roughly $3 to the economy, supporting the innovation workforce in NY-03 where firms like B&S Aircraft Alloys Inc. provide critical aerospace components. Sustaining this funding is vital to counter international competition and maintain the scientific talent pipeline that powers New York's technology sector.
fields.rationale: Connects the national $9 billion ask to local economic impact and workforce benefits, citing a specific contractor in the district.

fields.label: Project Name
fields.rationale: Identifies the specific NASA SMD program as the project name.

fields.label: Organization Name

Is your organization located within the NY-03 District?�

To find your district here:�https://www.house.gov/representatives/find-your-representative
Names and email addresses of all other individuals who should be included in correspondence about this request.�
Project Description
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to support Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences
* Funds mission development and operations for priorities such as Mars Sample Return, the James Webb Space Telescope, and the Earth System Observatory
* Implements scientific roadmaps established by the National Academies' Decadal Surveys to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration
* Counteracts rapid international expansion in space science by competitors like China to ensure the U.S. sets 21st-century exploration norms
fields.rationale: This description incorporates the requested funding amount, the directorate's core divisions, and key strategic arguments from the advocacy talking points regarding international competition and decadal priorities.

fields.label: What are the benefits of this project to the district? Why is it a priority?
fields.draft_value:
* Supports the local aerospace supply chain including B&S Aircraft Alloys Inc. in NY-3, which provides critical specialized materials for NASA flight hardware
* Stimulates regional economic growth; NASA science investments historically return roughly $3 to the economy for every $1 spent
* Bolsters the STEM talent pipeline in New York by providing inspiration and demand for high-skilled engineering and technology roles
* Ensures U.S. leadership in space science, a non-partisan priority that protects national prestige and advances practical applications like weather forecasting
fields.rationale: This links the request to a specific recipient in NY-3 (B&S Aircraft Alloys Inc.) and utilizes the 3-to-1 ROI talking point to demonstrate local and national economic value.

fields.label: Estimated start and completion dates.
fields.draft_value:
* Project Start: October 1, 2026
* Project Completion: September 30, 2027
* Note: This request covers the FY 2027 federal fiscal year for ongoing programmatic operations and mission support
fields.rationale: As a programmatic request for FY 2027, the dates correspond to the standard federal fiscal year cycle.

fields.label: Funding amount requested (please email a detailed budget to SuozziAppropsFY27@mail.house.gov and Cindy.Rogers@mail.house.gov)
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000
* This request represents a $1.75 billion increase over the FY 2026 enacted level to restore workforce stability and accelerate scientific discovery
fields.rationale: Directly uses the $9B funding ask and the net increase calculation provided in the SMD topline funding history.

fields.label: Total project amount
fields.rationale: Uses the total strategic funding ask amount for NASA Science.

fields.label: Can you accept partial funding?
options: Yes
No
fields.rationale: While the full request is $9 billion, NASA Science can scale operations based on the enacted level, though the requested amount is necessary to meet decadal priorities.

fields.label: Is your organization a 501(c)(3) non-profit or a government entity?
options: 501(c)(3) non-profit
Government entity
fields.rationale: The Planetary Society is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization.

fields.label: Can your organization meet any matching fund requirements?
options: Yes
No
fields.rationale: Programmatic requests for federal agencies do not typically involve matching funds from the advocate organization.

fields.label: Does your organization already receive federal funding? If so, please list the amount and source.
fields.rationale: This requires information about the specific organization submitting the form.

fields.label: Can you provide proof of community support for the project (e.g. letters of support from community groups, elected officials, etc.)
options: Yes
No
fields.rationale: The request is supported by the Planetary Society's large membership and allied organizations like AAS and AGU.

Please include the project name and point person in the subject line.�
fields.draft_value:
* Supported by major scientific organizations including the American Astronomical Society (AAS) and the American Geophysical Union (AGU)
* Aligned with the House programmatic support letter led by Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
* Backed by the scientific community's consensus as documented in the National Academies' Decadal Surveys
fields.rationale: Provides evidence of broad scientific and congressional support as listed in the allied organizations and existing letter context.
